figures.caption: Fig. 12: Normalized area under the learning curve (higher is better). SACHI achieves the highest overall learning efficiency.
Fig. 13: Jump-start performance over the first 100K steps (percentage of best baseline’s final score). SACHI ranks first.

Fig. 1: Solving the MARL Information Bottleneck. (a) Each agent holds only a fragment of the information needed to determine the globally optimal joint action; (b) Since agents cannot see their teammates’ observations or intentions, their independent “rational” choices often lead to group failure; (c) SACHI resolves this by allowing agents to intelligently filter and “borrow” relevant context from teammates through graph transformer layers; and (d) Enriched internal representations allow agents to act independently at runtime while maintaining perfect joint alignment.
Fig. 9: Aggregate normalized scores with 95% bootstrap confidence intervals. SACHI leads on all four metrics; its CI does not overlap with any baseline on Mean, IQM, or OG.
Fig. 10: Average rank across five environments (lower is better). SACHI ranks first or second on every task.
Fig. 15: Ablation learning curves on REFERENCE. (a) Encoder architecture (parameter-matched). (b) Number of attention heads. (c) Layer depth. The default configuration is shown in red.
TABLE I: Final performance (mean ± stderr over seeds, last 2% of training). Best per environment in bold.
Fig. 14: Parameter breakdown per method. Blue: agent + mixer. Red: coordination module. SACHI has fewer total parameters than most baselines.
TABLE II: Test return (mean ± stderr over 5 seeds) at training milestones on REFERENCE. Best per column in bold.
Fig. 8: Learning curves across five cooperative environments (mean ± standard error over five seeds). SACHI (red, bold) matches or exceeds the best baseline on every environment by convergence.

Fig. 2: Overview of SACHI. Local observations are encoded into agent embeddings, refined through soft-attention-modulated graph transformer message passing, and mapped to per-agent Q-values. The model performs receiver-dependent information integration prior to action selection, allowing coordinated behavior to emerge under decentralized execution.
